description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that includes the docker-compose-common.yml file and defines three Docker Compose services: celery-worker, django-server, and testing-cypress. Use the Dockerfile at docker/Dockerfile with build targets celery-worker-nosrc, django-server-dbg, and cypress respectively, each with context ".." and the appropriate env_file entries (secret_locations.env, common.env, ${ENV_FILENAME}.env, and for testing-cypress also env/cypress-ibo-data.env). Configure celery-worker to mount the secrets volume at /run/secrets:ro and the repository at /home/container/workdir:rw. Configure django-server to run "/bin/sh -c \"python docker/wait_for/wait_for_postgres_60.py && python manage.py migrate && ${DJANGO_SERVER_SCRIPT}\"", expose ports ${DJANGO_PORT:-8000}:8000 and ${DEBUGPY_PORT:-5678}:5678, and mount the same volumes. Configure testing-cypress to depend on django-server, run "/bin/sh -c \"npx wait-on http-get://localhost:8000 && cypress run --headless -b firefox --spec \"${CYPRESS_SPEC_SCRIPT}\"\"", set environment variable ELECTRON_EXTRA_LAUNCH_ARGS=--js-flags=--expose_gc, hostname testing-cypress, image testing-cypress, ipc host, network_mode host, shm_size 4gb, mount the workdir volume, and set working_dir to /home/container/workdir/e2e. Ensure these services are started during the pipeline’s test stage.
